title: piper arboreum subsp. stamineum
intro: Piper arboreum subsp. stamineum, also known as the "Malabar Pepper" or "Indian Long Pepper," is a plant species belonging to the Piperaceae family, characterized by its long, narrow, and slightly curved fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of piperine, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Piper arboreum subsp. stamineum include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and dried fruit forms, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and immune function.
